description: Composite scenarios verifying an agent generates OneUI-correct code with @jds4/oneui-react. Each expectation is grep-able against the generated output.

evals.prompt: Build a product detail screen with @jds4/oneui-react: a rounded hero product image, a title, price, a primary 'Buy now' CTA, a secondary 'Add to wishlist' action, a row of filter chips, and a celebratory 'New' badge.
expectations: Primary CTA is a Button with attention="high" (not appearance="secondary")
Secondary action uses attention="medium" or attention="low" — not a second high/bold button
Filter chips use appearance="secondary"; the CTA does not
The 'New' badge uses appearance="sparkle"
Hero image is an Image (or Avatar) with alt and aspectRatio, rendered rounded — never a raw and never square
Icons come from @jds4/oneui-icons-jio via <Icon icon="..."> — no lucide-react/@tabler/@phosphor imports and no name= prop
Any non-default background is a <Surface mode=...>, not a <div style={{ background }}>
No hardcoded colors, px, or font literals; imports are from @jds4/oneui-react (never @oneui/ui or @base-ui/react)

evals.prompt: Create a settings form with @jds4/oneui-react: labelled Email and Password fields, a prominent search field, an error state on Email, and Save/Cancel actions.
expectations: Email and Password use the OneUI Input (labelled) — not a raw <input>
Input attention is "medium" or "high" only — never attention="low" on an Input
The search field uses shape="pill" (e.g. with type="search"); normal fields use the default shape
Save uses attention="high"; Cancel uses attention="medium" — appearance="secondary" is NOT used to demote a button
Email error is expressed via Input feedback props, not a hand-rolled red <div>
All styling via tokens (var(--...)) — zero #hex / px / raw font sizes; imports from @jds4/oneui-react

evals.prompt: Build a promotional hero banner with @jds4/oneui-react placed over a background image: a celebratory badge, a headline, body copy, and one CTA on top of the media.
expectations: On-media region wraps controls in a transparent-material <Surface> OR the lone CTA uses contained={false} — it does not paint an opaque box over the media
Exactly one bold/high focal point (the CTA); no competing bold elements
The celebratory badge/icon uses appearance="sparkle" and appears at most once or twice
Body text stays neutral (--Text-High/Medium/Low or the Text component); only links use a tinted/secondary appearance
The brand hero background is a <Surface mode="bold"> (or brand-bg) — not a literal background color
Image uses alt + aspectRatio and is rounded; icons only from @jds4/oneui-icons-jio
